title: cercocarpus betuloides var. blancheae
intro: Cercocarpus betuloides var. blancheae, also known as Blanche's mountain mahogany, is a shrub or small tree species that belongs to the family Rosaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Cercocarpus betuloides var. blancheae are said to include wound healing, reduction of fever, and relief from respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to address various health concerns.
